title: Coffee Grinder, Wancle Electric Coffee Grinder, Spice Grinder, One Touch Coffee Mill for Beans, Spices and More, with Clean Brush Black
description:
About this item
✅【A Secret To Grind Bean Effortlessly】Grinder comes with a pure copper motor and stainless steel blade. Easily observe the grinding situation by a transparent lid, extract the perfect flavor from beans. let you enjoy great coffee in a few seconds.
✅【Coarse Or Fine By Your Finger】Pour in up to 60g beans, put the lid on, press the control button, the grinding starts. Release the button, the grinding stops. Easy one-button operation of the grinder achieves you to get the desired coffee powders.
✅【Rich Aroma & Fresh Flavor】Pure copper motor, stainless steel blade & bowl. Meaning beans won't be burnt during grinding, and maintain a precise and consistent uniform grind. let you enjoy the rich aroma and fresh flavor.
✅【Save Your Precious Counter Space】Wancle coffee grinder offering a wide range of abilities. Excellent stainless steel blade chops ingredients through rapid spinning, so it not only works well with coffee beans but also works spice, seed, nut, herb.
✅【Easy life on Wancle 】Wancle is a trusted-worth brand. We believe that Wancle coffee grinder is an Indispensable important link in the chain that extends from the coffee cherry to an exquisite espresso. It would easier for you in this busy world.
✅ Voltage / Frequency: 120 VAC / 60 HZ, Product Power: 150 Watts
reviews_5s:
This is a powerful little grinder for a great price. It is small - it can grind up about 1/3 of a cup at a time. But it does it quickly and it does it very well. I use it to grind flax seed and it is able to grind them to a fine powder in less than 15 seconds. I have not used it to do coffee beans but I am sure it could handle that with no problems. It is noisy when in use but it is grinding after all. Easy to store and easy to clean. I just use a damp paper towel to wipe up inside as you can not submerge it in water. The blade is very sharp so you have to be very careful. This is a great grinder which can be used to grind many different things - seeds, nuts, coffee. I highly recommend!I recently started using a French press to make coffee, and I needed a coffee grinder that was easy to use on the daily. This little guy from Wancle is exactly what I was looking for. It's small, compact, and simple. I like my grind ultra fine, and I love that it takes less than a minute to achieve. I've had it for a little over a month now, and it's always produced a smooth, consistent grind every time.

So far, I've experienced no issues with it. The device does get a tad bit warm after each use, but it's nothing to be worried about. Grind has never seeped through the bottom of the device, so it doesn't create a mess, and cleaning it is very straightforward. Use the little brush to sweep out excess product from the device, wash the cap, and voila, you're done.

The cord is short, but I didn't find that to be an issue. If you don't have an easily accessible outlet near your countertop, keep in mind that the cord is around 15-20 inches (I'm guesstimating). In terms of noise, I wouldn't call it quiet because it does make a noticeable humming sound, but compared to a blender, it's a mouse.

I'm so glad I purchased this coffee grinder. If you don't need anything fancy but want something reliable that produces consistent results, buy this.Noisy, but grinds well for small batches. Recommend if s.all amounts are needed.Cannot grind super finely, but considering the price, a good grinder.This little grinder does a fantastic job of grinding beans evenly.This coffee grinder does the job, but I don't understand why people would bother grinding their own beans when ground coffee is available. I bought this because I bought a bag of beans thinking they were ground, and I wanted to use them up. After I ground up the whole bag I threw this grinder away because if I'm careful I'll never buy a bag of coffee again it it isn't already ground.I love this little grinder so much I had to leave a review for anyone looking for a small coffee grinder that is amazing! It gets used every single day, multiple times a day, and still going strong! And for the price?! Super pleased and so glad I got it!It had been a long, noisy, chaotic, holiday-event-attending day. I wanted some coffee and the new grinder was here!!! Got it out of the box, cleaned it out, put coffee in, (steel myself for a loud buzzzzz after all the chaos of the day)..... it was SO QUIET!!! And only a few pulses and coffee was all ground to my liking, ready to brew!!!!! This is awesome!!! Thank youYou decide if you want course or fine grade just by holding the grind button for a short or longer period.will grind fennel seeds, wasnt sure because they are so light
reviews_1s:
It's advertised as being for different products and having different settings - neither is true.

The "settings" are how long you hold the button down (ie, 10secs coarse, 20 less coarse, etc). That said, when you hold it down - whatever you're grinding just sorting of spins at the top and it ends up halfway too grinded and half not grinded at all.

Now onto the claim that it's meant for more than coffee (which is what I needed it for)... The box blatantly says it's a coffee bean grinder and the directions are only for coffee beans. Okay, fine I thought, I'll trust the listing and test it anyway. I ground up some red peppers and like I said above - half ground. Took me 10mins of messing with it to get the powder mostly even.

Now onto the reason I finally decided that I'm returning it... Cleaning it completely is near impossible. It comes with a little brush to dust off whatever doesn't come out, which is A LOT. Anything you grind sticks to the inside and since the blade is stuck to the large part (motor and all) - there's no way to wash it well. So, if you want to grind spices and then coffee, be prepared for spicy coffee. So, looks nice, crap performance.Like all coffee mills I've used, this grinder is supposed to start when the lid is pressed down to cause a plastic pin in the lid to trigger an button inset into the body to trigger the motor. It didn't start at first. I thought the kitchen circuit breaker had switched, but that wasn't it. It finally started after I made repeated efforts to adjust the lid by twisting it to find the exact, correct alignment of the pin (lid) and inset button (body). The trigger-button that's inset into the body is very small, less than 1/8", so the pin meant to be inserted to push it is tiny. (See photo.) That it doesn't reliably start without fine-tuning is a problem, but worse is that I have no doubt at all the tiny pin is fragile, and that the brittle plastic it's made of will snap off eventually from being very slightly misaligned and pushed off-kilter onto the body.Like how much quieter it is compared to old one I replaced and coffee comes out great . Grinder did not seem powerful but did job. For only 2 months and it broke . Stopped grinding in middle of my beans getting ground. Do not buy unless you just need a grinder for a few uses. Breaks after a few uses. I just started using it a couple weeks ago and just broke. There seems to be no warranty either. So choose something else. Cheaply made Chinese product. Not worth it. Don’t do that to yourself or to anyone else. Was poorly made. No good. Anything else I need to say to convince you. Trust me on this product is trash. Other grinder I got from Heb lasted me years!!! And was also under 15 bucksOkay I very very rarely do reviews because everyone’s experience is going to be different. I urge you to read the one star reviews though. I was so excited for this product because I accidentally bought whole beans. I was rushing around and annoyed with the normal Walmart crowd and just grabbed a bag without reading. So I bought this and was so excited for coffee. I used it once, and I didn’t overfill it because I didn’t want to break it. Let me tell you I should have overfilled it. Now I’m sitting here with a little bit of coffee grounds and a useless coffee bean grinder. The thing stopped working after first grind. Now I am sad and out of money that I thought would be useful. Don’t buy it. Especially if you don’t want to be sad.The Wancle Coffee Grinder works perfectly for a few months and then starts to fail. The grinder stops working when you press down to pulse the coffee beans. No amount of coaxing, begging, or pleading will get it to work. Then there's the endless cycle of reaching out to the seller (who are impossible to reach) to look for clues on how to fix the darn thing. You'll be told to pulse rice (but it won't pulse), which supposedly cleans it, etc. For the record, I clean it with the brush provided after each use. I only use it for coffee beans. I don't overfill it. I used to own a simple old coffee grinder but hadn't ground beans in years and thought I'd never need to again. I wish I hadn't given it away. It's a simple thing with a simple motor. Why can't manufacturers make things that work? There is no way to return it after the return window closes. Do not buy this model. I would give this product a five-star rating if it didn't fail.This is a nice unit. I use it for all sorts of things but it gets really hot. So you have to let it cool between usage. Meaning you can’t do multiple batches of anything else it gets hot and I’m concerned it will stop working.

Update:

I should have listened to the negative reviews. I purchased this item in ‘March 2024 and here it is Thanksgiving day 2024 and I’m trying to grind some chunky cinnamon pieces…and guess what?!!! The motor just hums!!! Just 8months later and it no longer works!!!! I am highly irritated. These marketplace sellers sell items that last for a few months then they often stop working or functioning properly in less than a year and since its marketplace there’s usually no manufacturer warranty. How frustrating!!! Luckily I have a mortar and pestle AND I have ordered a more reputable brand from target for $6 more.I bought this product because the price was good and It has a stainless steel bowl and blade. I use it to grind coffee beans. The bowl is too small. It holds enough for one cup, maybe two....and the way it is designed, you are likely to spill some of that when putting it into your coffee making device. I like my coffee ground down to a fine powder but with this device it takes a long time and it never really gets fine enough. It is not easy to use and doesn't give a good result. It might be ok as a spice grinder, but not for coffee.Now this is embarrassing. Bought as a gift. When recipient opened gift it was apparent the this coffee grinder had been previously used. It was out of the inner packaging, cord tie was at the bottom of the box and water marks were present bc it had been washed.Sadly it did not work. Did not want to turn on after trying different outlets (I thought it was the outlets) however after trying multiple times the grinder did not want to turn on.
I made sure it was on properly but never wanted to grind :(Excited to grind coffee beans waited few days after I ordered. It came and unboxed plugged it and motor wouldn't start. Definitely sending back. Suggest buying USA products! Disappointed for sure. When will I learn.
